question: The chart shows the distribution of energy use by source. Based on this data, which of the following conclusions can be made?

options.A: The share of Renewables is roughly equal to the combined share of Nuclear and Other.
options.B: Renewables accounts for less than one-third of total energy use.
options.C: The combined share of Fossil Fuels and Hydroelectric is smaller than the share of Other.
options.D: Nuclear is the smallest single energy source.

explanation: This pie chart uses dual encoding: slice angle represents percentage, and the radius also varies with percentage, so slice area differences are exaggerated. That can make the largest category look overly dominant. Renewables: 40%; Other: 23%; Hydroelectric: 20%; Fossil Fuels: 10%; Nuclear: 7%.
